question: Explain why an O-H peak disappears after shaking with D2O.
full_answer: The O-H proton is exchangeable. It is replaced by deuterium from D2O, and deuterium is not detected in the same way in a proton NMR spectrum. Therefore the O-H peak disappears.

question: Explain why iodine has a different concentration in two immiscible solvents at equilibrium.
full_answer: Iodine has different solubilities in the two solvents because the intermolecular interactions are different. At equilibrium the solute is distributed between the two layers, giving a constant concentration ratio at a fixed temperature.

question: Explain why CDCl3 is used instead of CHCl3 as the solvent for proton NMR.
full_answer: CHCl3 would give a proton NMR signal from the solvent. CDCl3 does not give the same proton signal, so it does not interfere with the spectrum of the sample.

question: Explain why deuterated solvents are used in proton NMR.
full_answer: A solvent containing ordinary hydrogen would give its own proton NMR peak. A deuterated solvent avoids this because deuterium does not give the same signal in a proton NMR spectrum.
